title: Multiline blocks should be enclosed in curly braces
description: Multiline blocks should be enclosed in curly bracesAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityCode SmellcweWhy is this an issue?More InfoHaving inconsistent indentation and omitting curly braces from a control structure, such as anifstatement orforloop, is misleading and can induce bugs.This rule raises an issue when the indentation of the lines after a control structure indicates an intent to include those lines in the block, but the omission of curly braces means the lines will be unconditionally executed once.The following patterns are recognized:if (condition) FirstActionInBlock(); SecondAction(); // Noncompliant: SecondAction is executed unconditionally ThirdAction();if(condition) FirstActionInBlock(); SecondAction(); // Noncompliant: SecondAction is executed unconditionally
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: Single-line statements on same line as control structure
logic.reasoning: Uses custom function to analyze source code indentation patterns to detect control structures (if, for, while, foreach, else) that lack curly braces but have misleading indentation suggesting multiple statements should be in the block. The function examines line indentation to identify cases where the second and third lines after a control structure are both indented more than the control structure itself, indicating potential confusion about which statements are executed conditionally.

should_trigger: for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)\n    Console.WriteLine(i);\n    Console.WriteLine("Done");
